file: 1526_Kemp 等 - 2024 - Production of Aviation Fuel-Range Hydrocarbons Through Catalytic Co-Pyrolysis of Polystyrene and Sou.txt

experiments.description: 在450°C、500°C和550°C下，单独热解聚苯乙烯（PS）和南方松木（Pine）
experiments.text_snippet: the catalytic co-pyrolysis of polystyrene and pine with red mud (bauxite residue) and ZSM-5 catalysts at temperatures of 450°C, 500°C, and 550°C was investigated

experiments.description: 使用赤泥（Red Mud）和ZSM-5催化剂，在450°C、500°C和550°C下，对聚苯乙烯和松木进行催化共热解
experiments.text_snippet: catalytic co-pylysis of polystyrene and pine with red mud and ZSM-5 catalysts at temperatures of 450°C, 500°C, and 550°C

experiments.description: 在没有催化剂的情况下，在450°C、500°C和550°C下对聚苯乙烯和松木进行共热解，以研究协同效应
experiments.text_snippet: The co-pyrolysis of pine and polystyrene without a catalyst produced on average 17% or 36.3 mg more kerosene-range hydrocarbons than predicted, with a maximum yield of 266 mg of C7–C17 aromatic hydrocarbons per gram of feedstock (26.6% conversion of initial feedstock) obtained at 550°C.

Catalyst loading.original: 10 wt% (4.5 g catalyst per 40 g feedstock)

Product yield.original: {'Polystyrene pyrolysis oil yield': '88.5-90.5% (no catalyst), 86.25-90.0% (red mud), 82.5-86.25% (ZSM-5)', 'Pine pyrolysis oil yield': '5.06-6.04% (no catalyst), 5.06-5.32% (red mud), 4.28-5.08% (ZSM-5)', 'Co-pyrolysis oil yield': '41.21-45.04% (no catalyst), 41.21-44.22% (red mud), 38.96-42.94% (ZSM-5)', 'Maximum kerosene-range yield': '620 mg/g feedstock (62% conversion) with ZSM-5 at 550°C', 'Co-pyrolysis kerosene-range yield': '266 mg/g feedstock (26.6% conversion) at 550°C without catalyst'}

Product Selectivity.original: {'Polystyrene oil composition': 'Mainly styrene, ethylbenzene, naphthalene, phenanthrene', 'Pine oil composition': 'Furans, furfurals, cyclopentones, phenolics, oxygenated compounds', 'Co-pyrolysis oil composition': 'Reduced oxygen content, increased monoaromatic hydrocarbons, reduced PAH compared to catalytic PS pyrolysis', 'ZSM-5 effect': 'Increased toluene and ethylbenzene, decreased styrene to 41.5%', 'Red mud effect': 'Increased oxygen-containing compounds in pine oil'}

experiments.experiment_description: 在450°C、500°C和550°C下，单独热解聚苯乙烯（PS）和南方松木（Pine）
experiments.text_snippet: the catalytic co-pyrolysis of polystyrene and pine with red mud (bauxite residue) and ZSM-5 catalysts at temperatures of 450°C, 500°C, and 550°C was investigated

Catalyst composition.original: {'Red Mud': 'Contains high concentrations of Fe₂O₃ (hematite), specific surface area of 21.92 m²/g, average pore size of 6.15 nm', 'ZSM-5': 'Silica-to-alumina ratio (SiO₂/Al₂O₃) of 38, minimum specific surface area of 250 m²/g, average pore size of 5 Å, contains 30% binder by weight'}

Catalyst loading.original: 10 wt% (4.5 g catalyst per 40 g feedstock)

Product yield.original: {'Polystyrene pyrolysis (max oil yield)': '90.5% at 550°C (no catalyst)', 'Pine pyrolysis (max oil yield)': '6.04% at 500°C (no catalyst)', 'Co-pyrolysis (max oil yield)': '45.04% at 550°C (no catalyst)', 'Co-pyrolysis (max C7-C17 yield)': '266 mg/g feedstock (26.6% conversion) at 550°C (no catalyst)'}

Product Selectivity.original: {'Polystyrene oil': 'Mainly styrene, ethylbenzene, naphthalene, phenanthrene, and related monoaromatic hydrocarbons (MAHs) and polyaromatic hydrocarbons (PAHs)', 'Co-pyrolysis oil': 'Higher proportion of monoaromatic hydrocarbons (MAHs) and significantly reduced PAH content (85% less than ZSM-5 catalyzed polystyrene oil)'}

experiments.experiment_description: 使用赤泥（Red Mud）和ZSM-5催化剂，在450°C、500°C和550°C下，对聚苯乙烯和松木进行催化共热解
experiments.text_snippet: catalytic co-pylysis of polystyrene and pine with red mud and ZSM-5 catalysts at temperatures of 450°C, 500°C, and 550°C

Product yield.original: 266 mg/g feedstock (26.6% conversion of initial feedstock) at 550°C

experiments.experiment_description: 在没有催化剂的情况下，在450°C、500°C和550°C下对聚苯乙烯和松木进行共热解，以研究协同效应
experiments.text_snippet: The co-pyrolysis of pine and polystyrene without a catalyst produced on average 17% or 36.3 mg more kerosene-range hydrocarbons than predicted, with a maximum yield of 266 mg of C7–C17 aromatic hydrocarbons per gram of feedstock (26.6% conversion of initial feedstock) obtained at 550°C.
